growth_records.strain: KSMBR1 (planktonic enrichment culture, ~97% K. stuttgartiensis by metaproteomics / 90% by metagenomics; NCBI genome LT934425.1)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (anammox) + 45 mM ammonium + 45 mM nitrite

up.compound: CO2 (dissolved inorganic carbon, net inorganic carbon fixation)
up.method: Estimated from growth rate x cell carbon content (0.0042 h-1 x 45 mmol-C gDW-1); used as anchor for 13C INST-MFA flux normalization

13C-formate tracing (50 mM pulse) showed direct assimilation via methyl branch of Wood-Ljungdahl pathway (M+1 acetyl-CoA/PEP/aspartate); assimilated, not fully oxidized to CO2; no per-biomass rate reported
Catabolic electron donor, 45 mM in feed; nitrite-limited operation; per-biomass anammox rate not tabulated in text (activity tracked via 15N2 production)
Catabolic electron acceptor, 45 mM in feed, kept nitrite-limited; per-biomass rate not tabulated
13C/2H-acetate tracing (5-10 mM) indicated K. stuttgartiensis does NOT assimilate/oxidize acetate in situ; acetate oxidation attributed to a low-abundance side-population organism

sec.method: Anammox catabolic end product (ammonium + nitrite -> N2); anammox activity confirmed via 14N15N production from 15N-nitrite; no per-biomass secretion rate reported in main text

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Net CO2 uptake (inorganic carbon fixation, MFA normalization anchor)
mfa.units: mmol-C/gDW/h (= 100 in normalized flux map, Fig. 4)
mfa.note: Estimated as growth rate 0.0042 h-1 x cell C content 45 mmol-C/gDW; all Fig. 4 fluxes reported relative to this = 100

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Ribulose monophosphate (RuMP) pathway contribution to fructose-6-phosphate synthesis
mfa.note: INST-MFA predicted RuMP (hexulose-6-P synthase + 6-phospho-3-hexuloisomerase) accounts for ~23% of F6P flux; including it improved model fit ~15% (SSR 839.9 vs 988.7)

13C-formate M+2 labeling of citrate/alpha-ketoglutarate confirms oxidative (not reductive) TCA operation despite no annotated citrate synthase; TCA runs incomplete, mainly to produce alpha-ketoglutarate
Detectable carbon flux; likely main NADPH source (no annotated transhydrogenase)
Main CO2/formate fixation reactions; high intracellular flux reproduced by INST-MFA

growth_records.cite: Christopher E. Lawson et al. Autotrophic and mixotrophic metabolism of an anammox bacterium revealed by in vivo 13C and 2H metabolic network mapping. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00805-w. [PMC8027424]
growth_records.snippet: Flux values were normalized to a net CO2 uptake rate that was estimated from the growth rate and cell carbon content: 0.0042 h−1 × 45 mmol-C (g DW)−1 = 0.186 mmol-C (g DW)−1 h−1. ... The media was supplemented with 45 mM of both ammonium an
growth_records.notes: 13C/2H INST-MFA study of chemolithoautotrophic anammox growth in a steady-state continuous membrane bioreactor. Growth rate mu = 0.0042 h-1 (stated by authors, consistent with reported SRT of 10.9 days: 1/(10.9 d x 24 h) = 0.00382 h-1; hydraulic retention 48 h gives hydraulic D = 1/48 = 0.0208 h-1, but biomass removal ~10%/day governs mu). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.0042 = 165.0 h. Net CO2 uptake computed by authors as mu x cell C content = 0.0042 h-1 x 45 mmol-C gDW-1 = 0.186 mmol-C gDW-1 h-1 (is_experimental=false: derived, not directly measured; serves as MFA anchor = 100 in Fig. 4 normalized map). Individual intracellular net flux values are given only in Fig. 4 (image) normalized to CO2=100 and in Supplementary Datasets 4-5, not in machine-readable body text; captured here are the CO2 anchor, the 23% RuMP contribution to F6P (SSR 839.9 vs 988.7, 95% CI), and qualitative directions of oxidative TCA/oxidative PPP/WLP. Formate is directly ASSIMILATED (not fully oxidized) via WLP; acetate is NOT used by K. stuttgartiensis in situ (side-population activity). Anammox catabolism: NH4+ + NO2- -> N2; no per-biomass catabolic uptake/secretion rates tabulated in the main text (activity verified via 14N15N from 15N-nitrite). Trace-elements composition cited from ref 43, not enumerated here.
